# Packd - Code & Command Explanation Log

*This document serves as an ongoing log explaining what the code and commands generated by Antigravity are actually doing behind the scenes.*

### Phase 1: Preparation
Currently waiting for confirmation on PostgreSQL installation before running the Next.js initialization terminal commands. 

*Once we run initializing commands (like setting up React, installing Bcrypt, etc.) and write the authentication logic, each step will be broken down and explained here!*

### The Initialization Command: `npx` vs `npm`
Before creating the project, it is important to understand the tool we are using:
*   **`npm` (Node Package Manager):** This is used to permanently *install* packages into your project (e.g., running `npm install bcrypt` downloads the password-hashing library into your `node_modules` folder so your code can use it forever).
*   **`npx` (Node Package Execute):** This is used to *run* a package once without permanently installing it globally on your computer. When you run `npx create-next-app`, it temporarily downloads the Next.js setup script, executes it to build your project skeleton, and then cleans itself up so it doesn't waste space on your machine.
